Theory shared with me: The short answer (the one nobody wants to hear) Because real power still lies with armed Chavismo, not the civilian opposition. And in difficult transitions, you negotiate with whoever can put out or start the fire, not with whoever has the moral high ground. ⸻ Now let's break it down layer by layer 👇 1️⃣ Why is Delcy Rodríguez at the table? Because Delcy represents three things that the US needs right now: 1. Administrative continuity • Ministries, PDVSA, banks, ports. • Without that, the country would be paralyzed in days. 2. Direct channel to hard power • Military, intelligence, colectivos. 	• She doesn't command, but she coordinates. 3. Ability to deliver something • Information, demobilization, signatures, orders. 👉 It's not sympathy. It's usefulness. ⸻ 2️⃣ Why not María Corina Machado? This hurts, but it's true: • María Corina doesn't control weapons 	• She does not control territory • She does not control logistics • She cannot guarantee that there will be no violence tomorrow And in a phase of conflict, that weighs more than: • Legitimacy • Votes • Popular support Furthermore (and this is key): 👉 For hardline Chavismo, she is an existential threat. Bringing her in now blocks any immediate negotiations. ⸻ 3️⃣ What about Edmundo González? Edmundo is: • an electoral symbol, • a figure of civil consensus, • but he is not a power broker. He is useful for later, not for putting out the fire. ⸻ The real logic (cold, but constant) Transitions always go through three phases: 🔥 PHASE 1 – Controlling the chaos Negotiations are held with: • those who have weapons, • those who can unleash violence, • those who know where the mines are. 👉 This is where Delcy comes in. Not for nothing. ⸻ 🧱 PHASE 2 – Power realignment The following begin to get involved: • civilians, • technicians, • “acceptable” actors. 👉 This is where Edmundo and others could come in. ⸻ 🗳️ PHASE 3 – Legitimization Yes, here: • elections, • María Corina, • democratic narrative. 👉 But this is at the end, not at the beginning. ⸻ The emotional mistake of Venezuelans Believing tha
